items.title: Crisis? What crisis? Starmer ally Steve Reed insists the PM is 'fine' as Burnham mounts leadership challenge after a wee
items.desc: Housing Minister Steve Reed insisted Sir Keir was 'fine' and getting on with the job in hand as he toured TV and radio studios today.

items.title: Reed: Starmer is Feeling “Fine” But “It’s Not a Good Week”

items.title: News24 | Israel threatens New York Times lawsuit over ‘most hideous and distorted lies’
items.desc: Israel threatened to take The New York Times to court over a piece it published denouncing allegedly widespread sexual abuse against Palestinian detainees.

items.title: Turks’ overseas property purchases fall in March amid war impact
items.desc: The amount spent by Turkish residents on real estate purchases abroad fell 18 percent year-on-year in March to $187 million, as the conflict between the United States, Israel and Iran weighed on property transactions.

items.title: PongBot Aura is a $499 AI ball machine for tennis, pickleball, and padel
items.desc: PongBot has launched Aura, a ball machine that physically reconfigures for tennis, pickleball, and padel from a single chassis. The company set an introductory price of $499, which lands at roughly half the cost of the cheapest single-sport competitor current…

items.title: Petrol, diesel prices hiked by Rs 3/litre: Here’s what top economists have to say
items.desc: Fuel prices in India saw their first increase in over four years on Friday. Petrol and diesel costs rose by approximately ₹3 per litre. This move by state-run oil companies aims to address losses from rising global crude oil prices. The increase reflects the …

items.title: Trump Projects Confidence on Iran War While China Stays Cautious
items.desc: President Donald Trump said the US and China share common goals for ending the Iran war, while Beijing struck a measured tone in urging further diplomacy.

items.title: Trump Departs Beijing After Summit Meeting With Xi—Claims They ‘Settled Lot Of…Problems’ (Live Updates)
items.desc: Trump heaped praise on Xi again and said both leaders feel very similar about the Iran war and “want it to end.”

items.title: Fatherland movie review: Sandra Huller shines in a haunting masterpiece of a divided Germany
items.desc: Fatherland movie review: Fatherland could loosely be considered a trilogy with ‘Ida’ and ‘Cold War’, even if it’s the first time the Polish auteur has portrayed a version of real-life characters.

items.title: IDF soldiers jailed for desecrating statue of Mary
items.desc: It's not the first time IDF soldiers have been disrespectful of Christian sites.

items.title: CIA chief makes historic trip to Cuba as US blockade chokes island's energy supplies
items.desc: The U.S. has been restricting oil shipments to Cuba since January, plunging the country into blackouts lasting days at a time.

items.title: Xi-Trump summit: reset for US-Chinese relations but tension over Taiwan remains
items.desc: The Chinese president warned of possible conflict if the issue of Taiwan is ‘handled poorly’.

items.title: Mizuho Expects Another Year of Record Profit, to Buy Back Shares
items.desc: Mizuho Financial Group Inc. forecast a third straight year of record profit, although it sees the pace of earnings growth slowing as the Middle East conflict casts a shadow over the outlook for Japanese banks.

items.title: MOE raises school bus operator support to 20% of fare revenue amid continued high fuel prices
items.desc: Should fuel prices remain elevated, MOE said transport operators will, from July, be allowed to offset higher operating costs through a "time-bound" fuel surcharge.

items.title: Trump Departs Beijing After Cordial Xi Summit but Deep Rifts on Taiwan and Trade Linger
items.desc: President Trump's visit to China featured symbolic gestures and discussions on trade and Taiwan, but failed to resolve key tensions. Business leaders joined talks, highlighting economic stakes.

items.title: Russia & Ukraine Swap 205 PoWs Each in Latest Exchange
items.desc: 205 Russian servicemen have been returned from territory controlled by the Kiev regime, in exchange for 205 Ukrainian prisoners of war, the Russian Ministry of Defense said.

items.title: Strong overseas demand lifts orthodox tea sales in Kochi auctions
items.desc: Strong overseas demand boosts Kochi's orthodox tea sales, with Russia and UAE as key buyers amidst rising prices and limited arrivals.
